findings.message: Hardcoded API secret committed in source code. The default value 'super-secret-key-do-not-share' is a literal secret embedded in the repository, making it accessible to anyone with read access to the source (including git history). Even though pydantic BaseSettings can override via APP_API_SECRET_KEY, providing a real-looking secret as the default means deployments that forget to set the env var will silently run with a known, shared, publicly-discoverable key — enabling auth bypass, token forgery, or signature compromise depending on its use.
findings.suggestion: Remove the hardcoded default. Declare the field as required with no default (api_secret_key: SecretStr) so Settings() fails fast when APP_API_SECRET_KEY is unset, and source it exclusively from the environment / secrets manager. Also rotate the leaked value and purge it from git history.
